sops.title: Collaborator SOP
sops.description: How humans and agents open compliant PRs against a PLX-tracked repo: the compliance gate, MC-Checkout discipline, audit mirror, and risk-tier bundles.
sops.audience: Anyone who opens PRs against a PLX-tracked repo (by hand or via an agent)

sops.title: Repo onboarding (fleet)
sops.description: Add a repository to the PLX-tracked fleet: register it in config/tracked-repos-registry.json, scaffold the thin governance layer (GOVERNANCE.md pointer, CONTRIBUTING stub, generated compliance + drift workflows) via scripts/scaffold-tracked-repo.sh, wire secrets/branch protection, then flip status to active.
sops.audience: Maintainers and org admins enrolling a new repo into PLX governance

sops.title: Company Skills SOP
sops.description: Install company-approved Cursor/Claude skills on your machine, refresh when the catalog changes, create personal skills, and propose new skills for the team via petralabx/skills.
sops.audience: PLX contributors using Cursor, Claude Code, or other agents on personal laptops

sops.title: Agent — How to Use Mission Control
sops.description: End-to-end agent guide: MC hierarchy, dual registries, MCP checkout/progress/complete, MC-Checkout stamps, task.evidence vs PR-body rollback, Loop Ledgers owner-aware reads, and risk-tier bundles.
sops.audience: Anyone driving an AI agent (Cursor, Claude Code, ChatGPT/Codex, swarm) against a tracked repo

sops.title: Human — How to Use Mission Control
sops.description: Human UI workflows: projects, buckets, tasks, accountable owners, Request/Approve repos, reviewing agent evidence, Loop Ledgers observatory, and when humans can skip checkout.
sops.audience: Human collaborators working in Mission Control directly

sops.title: Repo hygiene & evidence bundles
sops.description: Where files belong: approved root files, forbidden patterns, dated artifact bundles, and documented archives — derived from the repo hygiene spec.
sops.audience: Contributors and agents adding files, docs, or evidence to a tracked repo

sops.title: Rollback plan requirements
sops.description: What a Rollback Plan must contain per risk tier, when it is required, and how the gate checks for it on non-docs changes.
sops.audience: Anyone opening a PR beyond docs/tests on a tracked repo

sops.title: Fleet secrets & CI tokens
sops.description: How COMPLIANCE_CI_TOKEN, PLX_MC_BASE_URL, allowlists, and MCP keys are provisioned on GitHub Actions and the MC deployment — companion to the compliance-gate rollout and repo-onboarding runbooks.
sops.audience: Org admins wiring compliance on a tracked repo

sops.title: PLX-Brain — Ask the Brain first
sops.description: Daily read-before / write-after loop for PLX-Brain: brain_search (MCP/HTTP/UI), interpret score floors, brain_ingest with provenance + ladder tags, propose relations, and session-artifact capture. Pointer to the canonical SOP in petralabx/agentic-swarm.
sops.audience: Operators and agent runtimes consulting the company brain

sops.title: PLX-Brain — Department key provisioning
sops.description: Provision VMC_SCOPED_API_KEYS dept grants, verify 403 on out-of-grant namespaces, and the critical caveat that dept keys are not isolation until TASK-541 route wiring. Pointer to the canonical SOP in petralabx/agentic-swarm.
sops.audience: Operators provisioning department-scoped knowledge API keys

sops.title: PLX-Brain — Weekly health triage
sops.description: Monday ~10-minute ET checklist for Second Brain eval, lint, cron watchdog, and ingest status, plus alert playbooks and monthly quarantine/replay. Pointer to the canonical SOP in petralabx/agentic-swarm.
